195,IssuesEvent,2016-12-10 19:21:23+00:00,44054354,dabeaz/curio,350836,dabeaz,,,closed,"I think Socket.close should be a regular method, not an async method",[],[],13,"So obviously, you cannot call async methods from `__del__` methods. And because of this, `close` methods generally have to be non-async as well. And in particular, Python enforces that the generator protocol's `close` method is synchronous and cannot `yield`:

This doesn't come up much in the context of coroutines, because coroutines are usually iterated to exhaustion. But as soon as you have [asynchronous generators](https://pypi.io/project/async_generator/) then it's a real problem.

For example, imagine that we have an asynchronous WSGI-like API that wants to use an `async for` loop to collect up the body of an HTTP response. And suppose that we have some code that builds that body by reading from a remote socket::

And suppose that this iterator is _not_ exhausted -- for example, because the HTTP client went away, so the WSGI-like server code stopped iterating over it. Then eventually either the server code or the Python runtime will call `.close()` on the `get_html_body_from_socket` coroutine, this will inject a `GeneratorExit` exception, and we are required to handle this exception _without_ yielding. So the above code is... kinda wrong. You'll get away with it in practice because Curio's `Socket.close()` doesn't actually yield, but the easy general rule is that coroutine `finally` blocks should never contain `await`, and this violates that. And this constraint means that Curio's `Socket.close()` must never change in the future to yield, so we might as well make it non-async to remind us of this and enforce it.
For the same reason, I guess `Socket` should implement the synchronous `__enter__`/`__exit__` protocol instead of `__aenter__`/`__aexit__`.
Possibly there are other places in curio with the same issue.

,{},{}

The ""action"" schema node cannot have ""rpc"", ""notification"", ""action"", key-less ""list"" or ""case"" as its schema node ancestor. It may also not appear at top-level of a module.
>    An action MUST NOT be defined within an rpc, another action, or a
notification, i.e., an action node MUST NOT have an rpc, action, or a
notification node as one of its ancestors in the schema tree.  For
example, this means that it is an error if a grouping that contains
an action somewhere in its node hierarchy is used in a notification
>   An action MUST NOT have any ancestor node that is a list node without
>   Since an action cannot be defined at the top level of a module or in
a ""case"" statement, it is an error if a grouping that contains an
action at the top of its node hierarchy is used at the top level of a
module or in a case definition.
All ""uses"" statements in the following module are erroneous (except the one being augmented).

431,IssuesEvent,2018-03-12 18:17:32+00:00,22937749,rasbt/mlxtend,11656932,jrbourbeau,,,opened,Add markersize parameter to plot_decision_regions,[],[],0,"I recently needed to change the scatter plot marker size in `plot_decision_regions` to make the markers easier to see in a presentation. A relatively easy fix was to use the matplotlib style context manager to temporarily change the plotting style. For example,
plot_decision_regions(X, y, clf, colors='C0,C1,C2', markers='ooo' , ax=ax)

However, given that `plot_decision_regions` already has a `markers` and `colors` parameter for adjusting the marker style, I thought it would be natural to have a `markersize` parameter to more easily adjust the scatter plot marker size.
@rasbt does adding a `markersize` parameter to `plot_decision_regions` sound reasonable? If so, I can submit a PR. ",{},{}

The `INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES` property should be set in `<install-root>/lib/cmake/dlib/dlib.cmake`, but the code for it is missing.

I'm not really sure what causes it, since `<src>/dlib/CMakeLists.txt` calls `target_include_directories` with `$<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>` (that's what normally sets it). 595,IssuesEvent,2015-02-23 21:31:34+00:00,31230734,jaraco/keyring,308610,jaraco,,,closed,keyring package implements its own Python extension modules to interface to the various backends,[],[],0,The keyring package implements its own Python extension modules to interface to the various backends instead of using the standard Python interfaces provided by the various libraries. For example the gnome keyring is accessible via the ""gnomekeyring"" Python module.

Relying on custom extension modules also means that many developer tools are required to be available when building the package (a compiler as well as the development versions of the backend provider's libraries).
This file has also been filed downstream at https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/python-keyring/+bug/660819.

,{},{}

948,IssuesEvent,2017-12-20 16:43:33+00:00,667289,daler/pybedtools,17572426,scnorton,,,opened,cbedtools.create_interval_from_list crashes with IndexError on blank line,[],[],0,"Root cause of the crash is: https://github.com/daler/pybedtools/blob/63f5a444a03cab7a695c8ceb03416ca21710e980/pybedtools/cbedtools.pyx#L652

If a line in the bed file has fewer than 3 fields (chrom, start, end), then trying to access field 2 will expectedly create an IndexError.  Such is the case when an empty line (i.e. `line == '\n'`) reaches this point - clearly there are 0 usable fields in this case.  The BedTool iterator should explicitly skip blank lines, including those incurred after stripping comments, or invoke some other sanity check to ensure that the minimum number of BED fields exists in a given line.",{},{}

1669,IssuesEvent,2015-01-24 04:47:54+00:00,21961171,asweigart/pyautogui,10644362,ahuanguchi,,,opened,Fixable Hotkey Bug and Small Documentation Issues,[],[],0,"Hi, I thought I'd let you know that the hotkey() function doesn't always work on Windows. I managed to fix this issue in a similar keyboard automation script I was working on myself by adding the extended-key flag into the ctypes.windll.user32.keybd_event() function. For example, the following executes SHIFT + DOWN properly, which doesn't happen in pyautogui:
ctypes.windll.user32.keybd_event(0x28, 0, 1, 0)    # DOWN, third argument is KEYEVENTF_EXTENDEDKEY

I'm not completely sure why this needs the extended-key flag to work even though extended keys don't need the flag when used in isolation, but it makes certain useful hotkeys work properly. Additionally, adding the extended-key flag to key presses that don't need it doesn't seem to cause any issues, so the following also works:
Therefore, the `keyDown` and `keyUp` functions could probably be modified to include the extended-key flag.
Also, the current online documentation for message box functions includes defaultValue as an optional parameter when the functions actually accept default instead.

The page on keyboard control functions explains that it's possible to pass a list of keys into press() but then gives an example where keyDown() receives the list ['left', 'left', 'left'], which results in an AttributeError because it tries to use .lower() on the list.
I hope this submission is helpful!",{},{}

3768,IssuesEvent,2017-10-18 13:01:46+00:00,3877327,guardian/alerta,3237800,thehilll,164318,guardian,opened,Allow for configurable housekeeping?,[],[],0,"Moving from 4 to 5 the API endpoint for housekeeping is a nice change, but would it be possible to allow for some configuration (either in config file or via the URL)?

We have some alerts that fire fairly infrequently...in particular we have user laptop backups that send an OK on success.  This generally happens once a day, so with the default housekeeping they would get deleted fairly regularly.

The issue this causes is that the next day when the backup works and an OK is sent a Slack post is triggered (whereas if they are not deleted it would just be counted as a duplicate).  In v4 we just modified the suggested housekeeping script to expire closed alerts after 25 hours, but with housekeeping now internal to Alerta that's a little more cumbersome.
We could also modify our alert flow (we could easily just skip posting to slack on the success in this case), but this seems like something worth considering.
I believe the code at issue (for Postgres) is:
WHERE (status IN ('closed', 'expired') AND last_receive_time < (NOW() at time zone 'utc' - INTERVAL '2 hours'))
OR (severity='informational' AND last_receive_time < (NOW() at time zone 'utc' - INTERVAL '12 hours'))
We don't need infinite flexibility, using variables for the `2` and the `12` would be plenty.

4577,IssuesEvent,2016-05-20 14:01:59+00:00,30921526,mozilla-services/cliquet,250019,tarekziade,1066228,mozilla-services,closed,Uniformize the naming of getters in our classes,"[{'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/mozilla-services/cliquet/labels/easy-pick', 'name': 'easy-pick', 'color': 'fbca04'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/mozilla-services/cliquet/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb'}]",[],1,"I've seen that the different contributors to the project don't have the same way to define *getters* on classes.

For instance, on `resource.ViewSet`, I've seen that there are getters prefixed by `get_`, whereas in the *permissions* backend, it's not the case (we have methods named `object_permission_principals` for instance).
In the case of the ViewSet, the design is such that the class can have class attributes (which aren't prefixed by get, because they're attributes) and then getters which start by `get`.
I would advocate we try to follow this pattern everywhere, or update how we do it for viewsets. Thoughts?",{},{}

4672,IssuesEvent,2015-01-18 17:37:46+00:00,7491428,zopefoundation/zope.schema,242750,tseaver,1163184,zopefoundation,opened,`zope.schema` does not handle well nested object fields,"[{'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zopefoundation/zope.schema/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929'}]",[],0,"In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.schema/+bug/969350, @alexgarel reported:
> Using trunk version of zope.schema, if I use an Object field whose schema has a `Set` (or any `Collection`) of `Choice`, it fails validating.
> This is due to a bad (inexisting) handling of bind in `Object` field.
> svn commit 112445 [1] solve the bug for the case a a `Choice` inside an `Object`, but not in the case of a `Choice` inside a `Set` inside an `Object`.
> I propose a solution which handles binding not only validation.
> There is a difference with previous code : it does not bind inner `Choice` to `Object` value but to Object context.

> I prefer this solution since binding to value maybe problematic in a lot a cases, eg. when vocabularies depends on object situation in the object tree whereas most of the time, at creation, `Object` value is not yet tied to any context.
> I precise last point for eventual discussion:
> The main point is that my fix propose to bind `Object` field (as its done for collections, or vocabularies) but it introduce a difference with previous behaviour (itself shipped with 3.6.4)
> Previously, a `Choice` field nested in a `Object` field gets bound to the new value to be assigned to `Object` field at validation time.
> In the fix that I propose. I bind the `Choice` to the same context as the `ObjectField`.

> This fix which handles `Object.bind` feels more general to me than an exception in `_validate_fields` for `Choice`s. It already solves the problem of binding nested `Set` fields, and will permit a better handling for third party fields which need binding.

> I also prefer this solution since binding to value maybe problematic in a lot a cases, eg. when vocabularies depends on object situation in the object tree whereas most of the time, at creation, `Object` value is not yet tied to any context.

,{},{}

4821,IssuesEvent,2017-05-23 13:37:49+00:00,24993138,google/brotli,1050652,aidanhs,1342004,google,opened,Performance cliff when using large available_in (regression),[],[],0,"Introduced in cdca91b6f59dd7632985667.

When compiling the given `test.c` file with `script.sh` (just put them both in the same directory and run `script.sh`), you should see the following timing differences on compressing a 100MB buffer of zeroes (before and after the commit above):

This is most obvious when using `BrotliEncoderCompress` (uncomment the relevant 6 lines in `test.c` and comment out the rest to verify), but I've managed to reduce the problem to demonstrate it with `BrotliEncoderCompressStream`.

The cause appears to be the use of a 'large' value of `available_in`. If you swap the commented `partsize` line to be `65536` (matching the buffer size used in the `bro` tool), the performance difference almost vanishes.
(git clone https://github.com/google/brotli.git $D1 && cd $D1 && git checkout cdca91b6f~1 && make lib)
(git clone https://github.com/google/brotli.git $D2 && cd $D2 && git checkout cdca91b6f   && make lib)

5216,IssuesEvent,2018-01-08 13:45:37+00:00,38744670,zalando/patroni,3407345,CyberDem0n,1564818,zalando,closed,Patroni can set initialize flag and lost ability to bootstrap.,[],[],10,"We have problems with our etcd installation. Because of that we encounter bug concerning initialize key creation in method `patroni.ha.Ha#bootstrap` . It tries to create new key `if self.dcs.initialize(create_new=True):` but fails many times because of ReadTimeoutError exception. But one of requests actually hit etcd and was successful. In next iteration patroni saw empty key instead of None and starts to wait master to bootsrap. When we found cluster in this state we check etcd data and found initialize key with empty value.
This bug should not be an issue in normal environment but still can happen.
Possible solution will be to create initialize key (or another key) with member name and proceed with initialization if initialize value equals member name.

5459,IssuesEvent,2018-05-18 20:46:25+00:00,29208937,spacetelescope/gwcs,1110361,nden,2751928,spacetelescope,closed,issues using gwcs with units,[],[],1,"[ ] fix `wcs_from_fiducial` to work with `SkyCoord` objects.
Before units in modeling worked gwcs did the following when inputs were quantities or SkyCoord objects:
- Converted the inputs to the expected units in the input coordinate frame
- Attached the units of the output frame to the result
With units available we have now more use cases to consider:
1.1 Inputs are quantities and are passed through the entire transform
1.2 Inputs are numbers - make them quantities using the units in the input coordinate frame.
This assumes the numbers are in the expected units but that's a valid assumption in all cases.
2. Transforms were not initialized with quantities
2.1 Inputs are quantities - we can do what we did before (if there's a way to find out whether the
model was initialized with quantities or not).
2.2 Inputs are numbers - use numbers as inputs.

2.1 raises the question of how to distinguish if a transform was initialized with quantities or not. @perrygreenfield suggested requiring a transform to have `input_units` if it was initialized with quantities. This sounds reasonable to me. So in this case, this is what needs to change
When a GWCS object is initialized:
- If forward_transform.input_units exists check that the transform input units are the same as input_coordinate_frame.unit and raise an error if not.
- Perform the same check for transform.return_units and output_frame.unit
When evaluating the WCS and inputs are quantities:

,{},{}

2. Create 'test_sub' pull subscription to that topic if it doesn't exist
3. Attempt to pull messages from the 'test_sub' subscription

### What did you expect to happen?

- `pubsub.Client().topic('test_topic').subscription('test_sub').pull()` in step #3 should fail with an Exception (because I've asked for the subscription 'test_sub' in the topic 'test_topic', not the 'test_sub' subscription in the '_deleted-topic_' topic)

2. BUT... Checking if the sub exists with `pubsub.Client().topic('test_topic').subscription('test_sub').exists()` returns... True
3. And, it can be pulled from via `pubsub.Client().topic('test_topic').subscription('test_sub').pull()`

5606,IssuesEvent,2016-02-03 01:49:12+00:00,11031844,uqfoundation/dill,321534,mmckerns,2855931,uqfoundation,closed,Dill fails on classes with __slots__,[],[],1,By default dill fails to serialize objects/classes that contains `__slots__` (more about slots [here](https://github.com/yasoob/intermediatePython/blob/master/__slots__magic.rst)):
with `RuntimeError: maximum recursion depth exceeded` - as far as I understand, it somehow tries to pickle `a` and think that it requires `A` and so tries to pickle it once again and so on.
The problem can be solved by passing `byref=True` to `dumps`, however I believe this shouldn't make any difference in such case.
If that can't be fixed anyhow, there should be at least an error similar to the one from `pickle` when trying to `pickle.dumps(A())`:
```TypeError: a class that defines __slots__ without defining __getstate__ cannot be pickled```

Normally, target.exports should be a list of Target objects, as they will be used in computing strict dependencies list, while payload.exports should be string literals, as they are used for computing fingerprint.

If we directly copy ""exports"" using this logic: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/task/simple_codegen_task.py#L219-L221, then in synthetic target, payload.exports will be a list of Target objects, causing payload.fingerprint() to fail.",{},{}

1. When users finish importing content, they sometimes click 'back' in their browser and expect to be able to import more content. However this doesn't currently work.

2. On the channel list page, existing channels only have a 'delete' button, but no 'import' button. In order to import more content from a channel, you need to first click 'import' at the top and then look for the channel again.
Recommended updates below. Feel free to iterate.
* The 'content selection' page for importing should have a dedicated URL that users can navigate to. This URL should include both the source (drive name or central server url) and channel ID.
* The main channel listing page should include an 'import' action along with 'delete' under an 'Actions' drop-down. Selecting import:
* first pops up the source selection dialog
* then navigates directly to the content selection page for that channel and source
Users are currently confused, and this should help alleviate that.
cc @christianmemije @khangmach @jtamiace let me know what you think",{},{}

This happens, I believe, since [setuptools' `find_packages` looks for packages (dir with `__init__.py` module)](https://pythonhosted.org/setuptools/setuptools.html#using-find-packages), and the `pytest_profiling.py` module should probably be specified in a `py_modules` argument to `setup.py`:
To get over this I either fix `setup.py` or manually copy `pytest_profiling.py` module to my virtualenv `site-packages` dir.

This happens when I run `py.test` with both `--profile-svg` and `--profile` arguments.

For some reason I don't understand, there's an error in the constants imports in [evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py). It appeared with [this commit](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/e1515b6b196a7fbdeb97657101a6eed62c1a1435) due to [this change](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/64328ecd60a8f50e3ab706c254efd30d6fec3363#diff-2c154d088c2a7f106917603a2fb872a4) to `evm/chains/mainnet/constants.py`.
You can recreate the error as follows.

Then check out [this commit](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/b6f251956105d97270afca462e1df98b2a6f4b7f) (which just changes the orders of the constants imports back to what they were before the fix) and you'll get an error:

The culprit appears to be [these imports](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py#L3-L9). For some reason, `from .constants import...` seems to define a module global `constants` pointing to the chain constants file.

Swapping the order of these imports (as [here](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/b6f251956105d97270afca462e1df98b2a6f4b7f)) makes the error go away but we should understand what's causing it in the first place.

6599,IssuesEvent,2018-01-07 11:11:11+00:00,26829379,VUnit/vunit,4018223,kraigher,6691399,VUnit,opened,Expected failure,"[{'id': 149161273, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/VUnit/vunit/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': True}]","[{'login': 'kraigher', 'id': 4018223, 'avatar_url': 'https://avatars1.githubusercontent.com/u/4018223?v=4', 'gravatar_id': '', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher', 'html_url': 'https://github.com/kraigher', 'followers_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/followers', 'following_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/following{/other_user}', 'gists_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/gists{/gist_id}', 'starred_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/starred{/owner}{/repo}', 'subscriptions_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/subscriptions', 'organizations_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/orgs', 'repos_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/repos', 'events_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/events{/privacy}', 'received_events_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/kraigher/received_events', 'type': 'User', 'site_admin': False}]",0,"I would like to add an expected failure mechanism to VUnit.  An expected failure means the expectation is that the test case fails. Failing the test case will thus be an OK result. if the test case does not as expected fail it shall be a NOT_OK result.
To guard against that an expected failure is causes by an unintended failure I would also to have this in the VHDL code:
-- If failure occurs before it is an unwanted failure
-- Expected failures can only happen in-between here
-- If failure occurs after it is an unwanted failures
If for some reason the user does not want this extra safety guards it can be disabled in Python via:
It might also be convenient to add comment pragmas to the vhdl code to indicate that failure is expected:
if run(""Test that does not work yet"") then -- vunit: expect_failure
-- The expected failure comment pragma is valid until the next if run(""..."")
if run(""Test that does not work yet"") then -- vunit: expect_failure unguarded
In case of test bench without test cases or if all test cases should fail:
For the test report the expected failures there are a number of alternatives:
- Write pass in green text for expected failure that happened
- Write fail in red text for expected failure that did not happen.
- Write fail_ok in green text for an expected failure that happened
- Write no_fail in red text for an expected failure that did not happen

6818,IssuesEvent,2017-05-16 14:46:04+00:00,18031064,hmmlearn/hmmlearn,5981644,chanansh,7037444,hmmlearn,opened,GMM competability with Scikit-Learn,[],[],0,The code used to have _gmm property inside the GMMHMM class containing a list of gmm models (per state). Now the entire GMMHMM was rewritten to be self contained without depending on scikit-learn. I advice to not follow this root but rather rely on the existing GMM implementation in scikit-learn. This is why:
1. hmmlearn users can enjoy progress done on the efficiency and features of the GMM class in scikit-learn

2. Once can use hmmlearn for a semisupevised scenario when you would like to pre-train some of the states using scikit-learn and then inject them into hmmlearn as starting point or as fixed states (not to be learned any more).

6835,IssuesEvent,2017-06-27 09:46:15+00:00,13258039,aio-libs/aiohttp,4658472,omnicognate,7049303,aio-libs,closed,Not waiting for connection_lost() when transport closed,[],[],7,## Long story short

aiohttp calls `close()` on the transport and then immediately discards it without waiting for `connection_lost()`. This is a problem for SSL transports, which have to shut down asynchronously, and it appears aiohttp only ""works"" with the standard library's SSL transport because of a bug in that transport.
aiohttp waiting for connection_lost() before discarding transport
Run this program and breakpoint `connection_made()` and `connection_lost()` in client_proto.py. The former is called, but not the latter.

The program appears to run successfully, but to convince you that something is severely amiss, add a `__del__` method to the class `_SelectorSocketTransport` in asyncio's selector_events.py and breakpoint it and that transport's `close()` method. You'll see that `close()` is never called and `__del__` gets called during shutdown, after the loop has been closed and the module finalised, because it still has a reader registered and is therefore leaked (while still waiting on I/O) in a reference cycle with the event loop.
If I'm understanding it right, the overall behaviour appears to be due to two bugs, one in asyncio and one in aiohttp:

1. When `close()` is called on the SSLProtocolTransport in asyncio's ssl_proto.py it initiates an asynchronous shutdown process, which should ultimately result in it calling `connection_lost()`. However, this doesn't progress for reasons I haven't fully figured out, so `connection_lost()` is never called. The shutdown process is clearly broken because it never results in `close()` being called on the underlying transport, which is leaked as shown above.
2. aiohttp doesn't wait for `connection_lost()`, so the hang that one would expect as a result of this never happens. By luck, the broken shutdown sequence never actually causes any errors.

I'm actually more interested in the latter, the aiohttp behaviour, because I've written my own SSL transport (based on pyopenssl), which does a correct asynchronous shutdown sequence. When aiohttp discards my transport without waiting for `connection_lost()`, it causes errors, which I don't think are my fault.
Tested on Windows and Linux with python 3.5.1 and 3.5.2 and aiohttp 2.0.7 and 2.1.0.",{},{}

### Describe what you wanted to do
I wanted to call the method `set_name()` on an EncodedField, and later on an EncodedMethod, to set the in-memory name for a method that had been set to a single-letter, presumably by ProGuard.
- is this error specific to a single file?
- if possible give a minimal working example where the error happens
Presuming you have an APK loaded as `a`, the DEX loaded as the DalvikVMFormat `d`, and that the APK has a class `ExampleClass` with field `a`

The field referred to as `d.C.LExampleClass.F.a` is now referenced as `d.C.LExampleClass.F.new_name` and shown as `new_name` whenever the field is used, such as when calling `str()` or looking up the Xrefs
- please report logs, tracebacks and other useful information
- if possible report the files you tried to analyze!

commit 9d519eaae6542 changed the call from `self.reload()` to `self._reload()`  EncodedField and EncodedMethod still have `reload` methods.  This same commit added `_reload()` methods to other classes in the file, such as `FieldIdItem` and `MethodIdItem`.  However, `EncodedField` and `EncodedMethod` did not have their reload functions changed.",{},{}

7397,IssuesEvent,2018-03-19 13:53:32+00:00,26876372,dateutil/dateutil,1377457,pganssle,9849410,dateutil,opened,Provide a mechanism to get the Olson name of a zone,"[{'id': 149492527, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': True}, {'id': 177050120, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/time%20zones', 'name': 'time zones', 'color': 'c7def8', 'default': False}]",[],0,"This may not always be possible, but I think it could be very useful, and the overwhelmingly most common case here is that `tzfile` objects are created by `tz.gettz(OLSON_NAME)`, where at *some* point we at least had this information. Worst case scenario we pass this directly to `tzfile` and expose it as something like `zone_name`.

It is also worth considering switching over `__str__` so that it returns the zone name. That breaks backwards compatibility, but I'm not sure how many people are relying on it in the first place...",{},{}

7398,IssuesEvent,2017-08-05 15:45:24+00:00,26876372,dateutil/dateutil,1377457,pganssle,9849410,dateutil,opened,Add testing submodule,"[{'id': 149492527, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': True}, {'id': 335791870, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/tests', 'name': 'tests', 'color': '5319e7', 'default': False}]",[],0,"I have a few little testing utilities in [dateutil/test/_common.py](https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/master/dateutil/test/_common.py). I think that maybe some of them should be made permanent, for example [`TzContextBase`](https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/master/dateutil/test/_common.py#L95) and its derivatives could be used. Might be useful for people who want to test their stuff under different time zones.

That said, the way I'm doing it (especially on Windows) involves actually changing the system time zone, which may be a bit overkill compared to just mocking out all the relevant parts that return time zone information. Still, it's a start.",{},{}

7407,IssuesEvent,2015-01-09 01:30:52+00:00,4235447,django-salesforce/django-salesforce,181098,philchristensen,9872531,django-salesforce,reopened,SalesforceAutoField does not inherit from fields.AutoField,"[{'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/django-salesforce/django-salesforce/labels/discussion', 'name': 'discussion', 'color': '009800'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/django-salesforce/django-salesforce/labels/upstream-bug', 'name': 'upstream-bug', 'color': 'f7c6c7'}]",[],4,"I'm running into an error running tests on a postgres (non-Salesforce) database. The issue is that django doesn't respect `SalesforceAutoField.get_internal_type`, and generates errors like the following:
IntegrityError: null value in column ""Id"" violates not-null constraint

Django is not properly viewing the field as an auto-increment primary key. I think this is caused by a bug in Django [here](https://github.com/django/django/blob/01ab84c61330ffa5ac87c637249611c5e5343e57/django/db/models/base.py#L800), where it checks whether the field is an instance of `AutoField` instead of checking that `f.get_internal_type() == 'AutoField'`. The Django source code seems to be inconsistent on this point, sometimes checking `get_internal_type`, and sometimes checking whether the field is an instance of `AutoField`. I'm going to submit a patch with test cases to Django, but an easy fix in `django-salesforce` itself would be to have `SalesforceAutofield` inherit from `fields.AutoField`.
Set your settings file to use a postgres db:
from salesforce import models as sf_models
Then run in the python shell run

Micropython paste mode, (which suppresses python auto-indent) is successfully triggered by CTRL+E in both screen and miniterm.
It presents as comprehensible and usable with GNU Screen, as invoked like `screen /dev/ttyUSB0 115200`...

column2: Ctrl-C to cancel, Ctrl-D to finish

column1: However, for some reason, a paste into Miniterm from within the same desktop terminal, and to the same UART shell on the same device, adds extra newlines or carriage returns into the terminal when copy-pasting, which has the effect of junking the readability of the pasted code. For example, this session invoked like`python3 -m serial.tools.miniterm --raw --encoding ascii /dev/ttyUSB0 115200`.
--- Quit: Ctrl+] | Menu: Ctrl+T | Help: Ctrl+T followed by Ctrl+H ---

column2: Ctrl-C to cancel, Ctrl-D to finish

column1: Is there some flag I can use to eliminate this behaviour, or is it somehow to do with the way Miniterm actually sets up pipes of characters and is somehow baked into the system?

I would really prefer to use miniterm compared to GNU Screen, for cross-platform compatibility as part of support packages (mostly authored in python) as part of the [vgkits.org](https://vgkits.org) initiative.
For example, I can wrap miniterm easily from within my tool suite.

Unfortunately, copy-pasting from a text-editor into the shell for interactive debugging and learning is part of the core way of working I would like to share with learners of python. Is use of Miniterm and use of paste mode across platforms destined to fail?",{},{}

7589,IssuesEvent,2018-01-24 13:07:33+00:00,86575060,linnarsson-lab/loompy,10656387,slinnarsson,10656411,linnarsson-lab,closed,Conventions for keeping track of creation- and modification-dates,[],[],5,"An unfortunate aspect of the HDF5 format is that opening a file makes the operating system treat it as modified, even if nothing changed and even if it is opened in read-only mode. On Windows this results in a changed modification date, and on Linux and OSX there is no distinction between modification or creation dates to begin with.

It would be useful to be able to keep track of *real* changes to loom files. This could be used to trigger automatic updates in various work-flows, for example. Having different levels of granularity about what was changed would also be useful here. It would also be useful if there was a convention for this, so that modification-detecting scripts from different groups would work with each other's files without much trouble. If the `loompy` library would inherently keep track of and update these modification tracking attributes, things would be even easier, since people would not have to think about it.

To give a concrete example: to serve data from a loom file to a website, a `loom-viewer` server must extract the data from the loom file and convert it to JSON. This is a relatively slow process, and on top of that h5py does not like it when an HDF5 file is opened by multiple processes (even in read-only mode). So to mitigate this issue, whenever JSON data is generated it is also cached as a zipped static file. The next time someone requests that data, the static file is served instead of repeating the whole process.

The problems start if the data in the loom file is modified (for example, when a column attribute is added to loom file). At this point, existing JSON files that are outdated have to be replaced. It is currently not possible to detect when to do this automatically - it needs to be done manually by whomever is modifying the loom file.

One way around this would be to have a global attribute, or multiple attributes, that are used to keep track of real file changes. Being able to distinguish different kinds of modifications would be nice too. For the `loom-viewer` the following level of precision is enough:
- the data matrix as a whole
But perhaps other people have a use for more fine-grained checks (detecting which rows were changed, for example).
I would like to hear the thoughts of others on this, and come up with a shared proposal for how to handle this.",{},{}

7782,IssuesEvent,2015-09-15 12:50:09+00:00,23924195,Lasagne/Lasagne,629706,f0k,11752110,Lasagne,opened,MNIST example: Pull data from original website,[],[],0,"As discussed in https://github.com/Lasagne/Recipes/pull/21, it's not a terribly good idea to have the MNIST example depend on the pickled MNIST dataset from deeplearning.net: (a) the website is down sometimes, causing Travis to fail for any pull requests submitted during that time, (b) asking users to download and unpickle a pickle is not a good practice ([anything could happen](http://www.cs.uic.edu/~s/musings/pickle.html) on unpickling a pickle).
We should change the MNIST example to download and load the data from Yann's original website: http://yann.lecun.com/exdb/mnist/
It's relatively easy to read using a combination of `gzip.open`, `np.fromfile` and `np.reshape`, you just need the correct dtype, offset (to skip the header) and shape.
I also wrote a generic MNIST format reader some time which can be used as a reference (it can be simplified a lot since we can just hard-code the shape and dtype):
raise ValueError(""This is not a valid MNIST matrix file."")

7922,IssuesEvent,2017-09-10 13:25:23+00:00,65030656,natasha/yargy,153776,alexanderkuk,12713890,natasha,opened,[WIP][RFC] Новые пайплайны,[],[],0,"> Текст тикета не закончен и будет дополняться

1. Бывает забываешь добавить пайплайны при инициализации парсера. Парсер мог бы сам добавить пайплайн или хотя бы кинуть ворнинг если где-то в грамматиках встречается предикат типа `gram('CustomGram')`  , а соответствующего пайплайна нет в аргументах парсера.

2. Можно ошибиться в предикате gram. Написать например не `gram('CustomGram')` а `gram('Custom')`. Такое происходит не из-за опечаток, а когда начинаешь менять код в разных местах. В пайплайне заменил grammemes а в грамматиках забыл.

7948,IssuesEvent,2016-02-11 03:06:30+00:00,18188537,dronekit/dronekit-python,5368500,hamishwillee,12973369,dronekit,opened,Make all attributes read-only and have explicit setter methods,[],[],0,Most attributes are read-only but we have a number of writable attributes: ``Vehicle.mode``, ``Vehicle.armed ``, ``Vehicle.airspeed`` and ``Vehicle.groundspeed``, ``Vehicle.home_location``.

There is nothing wrong with writable attributes ""in general"" but in this use case they aren't great because they imply attributes can be written synchronously and immediately ... and in our application they can't. This leads to ""hey what"" moments, means that we need to write somewhat complicated code to check the success of a write, and makes it easy to miss these checks.

The proposal is to make all the attributes read-only and move to explicit setters. The setters would be asynchronous, but provide wait_ready to allow synchronous use, timeout and callback for handling a fail case (with an exception being raised if there is no callback defined):
# Confirm vehicle armed before attempting to take off
# Confirm vehicle armed before attempting to take off

8194,IssuesEvent,2016-12-16 15:27:54+00:00,43852872,PulpQE/pulp-smash,16638409,goosemania,14839747,PulpQE,opened,Test metadata consistency checks for RPM repo,[],[],0,"Redmine issue: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/1287
Sync task should fail early in the process (before content is downloaded) complaining about malformed repository in following cases:

- one of the necessary metadata files is missing (this can be the case for gitlab repositories because they use their own tool similar to `createrepo`). Necessary ones are `primary`, `other` and `filelists`.
- `filelists.xml` and `other.xml` contain metadata for different number of packages.
This can also be the case for gitlab repos, they intentionally do not generate content for `filelists.xml` sometimes but the file itself may be present, just without any packages.
We also observed this issue on our CDN.
- `filelists.xml` and `other.xml` does not contain metadata for all the packages, aka the number of packages is not the same as for `primary.xml`.
We observed this issue on our CDN. ",{},{}

8374,IssuesEvent,2018-06-15 07:19:36+00:00,55808787,openthread/openthread,5144764,LuDuda,16142269,openthread,opened,[ip6] memory leak when passing frame from NCP to Host,[],[],0,Hello,
Recently I have found a serious issue while performing OpenThread NCP stress tests.
I have 24x NCP devices (nRF52840) in my setup that flood network (approx. for ~8h) with Echo Request packets on FF03::1 with different sizes. I control devices using pyspinel wpan API.
The result i found is that after test (or in the middle of the test) i see a flood of _MLE Parent Request_ messages. The end result is that all devices become Thread Leaders.
I saw also some posts from other users that discovered this problem with a 2-3 devices in the network.

I simply rebuilt firmware without optimization and put breakpoint in the `MleRouter::HandleParentRequest`, then i found that `MleRouter::SendParentResponse` fails because there is no free buffers (even couple of hours after performing stress tests).
To find a memory leak i have added profiling method to `Message` class e.g:
Also i set `ConsumerFuncName` to NULL in `MessagePool::Free` method.
_Side Note:  I don't feel this profiling method is the greatest, though i haven't find better way. Anyway, do you think that this kind of profiling has potential to be a part of OpenThread?_
After problem occurred again i printed logs from the GDB, and voilà, a lot of buffers pointed to `Ip6::ProcessReceiveCallback`:

mConsumerFuncName = 0x5a9e0 <ot::Ip6::Ip6::ProcessReceiveCallback(ot::Message const&, ot::Ip6::MessageInfo const&, unsigned char, bool)::__PRETTY_FUNCTION__> ""otError ot::Ip6::Ip6::ProcessReceiveCallback(const ot::Message&, const ot::Ip6::MessageInfo&, uint8_t, bool)""
Anyway the strange thing is that when i use `otMessageGetBufferInfo` i see that all buffers all free?!
I am not sure yet where the problem is, i have two candidates and i am going to check them, however i would appreciate if you @jwhui can look on this as well.",{},{}

The main difference is that it does not have a `.get()` method, the equivalent being named `.getPointer()`. It has the expected `operator*` and `operator->`.

8748,IssuesEvent,2016-11-07 12:50:56+00:00,50646767,raiden-network/raiden,107070,utzig,19666847,raiden-network,opened,Performance issues on LogListenerTask,[],[],0,"LogListenerTask should be improved to minimize the number of RPC calls done.

Inside Raiden there are 5 ou 6 tasks created using a LogListenerTask, each for a different event (e.g new channel, channel settled, etc). Each of these tasks poll `eth_getFilterChanges` every 0.5s (by default). This amounts to a lot of RPC calls and the events that are polled for happen very sparsely.

An initial suggestion would be to add one single `FilterPollerTask` that polls for all topics that log listeners want to monitor and delivers the events to the appropriate `LogListenerTask`. This should already reduce any number of event pollers to a single RPC call.
Later some form of caching should probably be employed.",{},{}

8804,IssuesEvent,2017-05-31 20:48:59+00:00,51625787,vatlab/SOS,9889312,BoPeng,20074389,vatlab,opened,DataFrame visualization as plugins.,[],[],0,"Currently `%preview --style` supports [`table`](http://vatlab.github.io/SOS/doc/documentation/Notebook_Interface.html#Preview-of-tables-144) and [`scatterplot`]( http://vatlab.github.io/SOS/doc/documentation/Notebook_Interface.html#Scatter-Plot-145), and it would be nice to handle this through the preview plugin system so that it is possible to develop different plots for dataframe.

One of the technical difficulties, however, is that such plots might need additional css. Although it is possible to inject css from JavaScript, the css can only be injected when the JS code is executed, so they might not be usable when a notebook is opened, or when the notebook is converted to HTML. We should either
1. Make sure css are injected all the time, or
2. Add JS to templates and kernel.js
3. Allow sos to load and use third-party css.
Option 3 seems to be the only feasible solution if we take a modular approach.",{},{}

8939,IssuesEvent,2018-02-02 07:56:44+00:00,71932349,ray-project/ray,249517,robertnishihara,22125274,ray-project,closed,How to know when an actor is available.,"[{'id': 468774489, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ray-project/ray/labels/question', 'name': 'question', 'color': 'cc317c', 'default': True}]",[],1,"Suppose we create a handful of actors, assign a task to each, and want to assign a new task to an actor whenever that actor becomes available. `ray.wait` allows us to know when an ""object"" is available, but what we really want is an easy mapping from the object ID to the actor object.
1. If the actors are stored in a list, then pass the actor's index in the list into the actor constructor and return it from the relevant method.
2. Maintain a dictionary mapping object ID to actor.

Note that in a lot of these situations, the actors are considered interchangeable for the purposes of the application, so this might be addressed by creating ""stateless"" actors whose identities don't matter and where multiple replicas of the actor are hidden behind a single object that we call methods on.",{},{}

9119,IssuesEvent,2017-08-04 06:32:48+00:00,35531022,opendatacube/datacube-core,12852539,benjimin,26125288,opendatacube,opened,Transferring between database instances,[],[],0,"Copying dataset records between different databases is tricky yet very useful (e.g. production vs private). I propose the documentation should explain the process in detail, or there should be a dedicated tool.

(The process is tricky because some of the relationships use keys that are specific only to the db instance, which means the data seem scrambled, e.g. datasets end up grouped under the wrong product, if naively inserted from one db into the other.)
One solution would be along these lines:
create server proddb foreign data wrapper postgres_fdw options (host 'agdc-db', dbname 'datacube')
create user mapping for $user server proddb options (user '$user', password '$pass')
import foreign schema agdc from server proddb into prod
create temporary table staging as select * from prod.dataset where dataset_type_ref=(select id from prod.dataset_type where name like '$product')
update staging set dataset_type_ref=(select id from agdc.dataset_type where name like '$product')
update staging set metadata_type_ref=(select metadata_type_ref from agdc.dataset_type where name like '$product')
insert into agdc.dataset select * from staging
insert into agdc.dataset_source select * from prod.dataset_source where dataset_ref in (select id from staging)
column2: --may fail here unless ancestors tranferred before derived products!

column1: insert into agdc.dataset_location select * from prod.dataset_location where dataset_ref in (select id from staging)

,{},{}
